column2: The Denver Parks and Rec Summer in the Parks camps are really great, especially if one parent has a slightly flexible schedule. City Park has a location from 9:30-3:30 but some of the others (Swansea) extend for an additional hour and they actually provide lunches. My oldest has done it for 3 summers (they didn’t have it the first Covid year). It’s incredibly economical: $550ish for all summer.

From our experience last year, our kiddos wanted to stick with the camps more than a week - but they loved all of them. We did a few bike camps with Pedalheads, a zoo camp, DMNS museum camp, DU Pass camps, and an avid 4 adventure camp. I am planning to get into any of these camps for a few weeks in a row this year instead of one week here and there. My kids LOVED zoo and Museum and then 2nd loved the pass and Pedalheads (in all fairness they have done lots of Pedalheads camps for many years, but it’s good to start the season).
